skill.name: viral-short-form
skill.description: Brainstorm and write high-retention short-form video and carousel content for TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts, and carousels. The skill helps users generate diverse hook options, structure scripts around hook, escalation, payoff, and CTA, critique weak drafts, and adapt content to platform-specific short-form patterns. It uses pattern-based guidance and explicitly avoids promising virality.
skill.summary: Generate hooks, scripts, carousels, and critiques for short-form content using retention-focused patterns.
skill.author: vyralcontent

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 73 potential issues across Markdown files, but review found no executable code, shell invocation, cryptographic implementation, system reconnaissance, prompt injection, or data exfiltration. The flagged items are Markdown code fences, backticked file names, relative documentation links, ordinary URLs, and prose about content strategy. The skill is safe to publish as instructional writing content.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives. The reviewed locations contain Markdown code fences, inline file references, companion skill names, and content templates, not Ruby shell backtick execution or any runnable command path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are Markdown-only instructional content. The matched backticks delimit examples or file names and are not interpreted as shell execution.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Prose Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives. The word weak appears in ordinary writing guidance, such as weak hooks or weak items, and no hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Semantic review found no crypto libraries, hashes, ciphers, credential handling, or security-sensitive computation. The matches are content-writing vocabulary.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Content Strategy Terms Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance findings are false positives. The matched text discusses recommendation algorithms, content systems, or content workflow, and does not inspect hostnames, users, processes, files, environment variables, or machine state.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are prose for short-form content strategy. No commands or instructions gather local system information.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Links Flagged as Network Access
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings are low-risk documentation links. README.md links to the SKILL.md standard and the author website, while SKILL.md embeds a remote header image and links to the author website; no code performs network requests or sends data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are static Markdown links or an image reference. There is no executable downloader, fetch call, webhook, telemetry, or credential transmission.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Relative Markdown Links Flagged as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The filesystem path traversal findings are false positives. README.md uses relative links to an image asset and license file within repository documentation, not runtime file reads or writes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The ../ sequences appear only inside Markdown links. No executable code consumes user-controlled paths or accesses the filesystem.

content.value_statement: Creators often lose viewers because hooks are vague and scripts lack a clear payoff. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured patterns for hooks, scripts, carousels, and critique.
actual_capabilities: Generates 6 to 10 hook options across distinct hook archetypes.
Builds scripts around hook, escalation, payoff, and CTA structure.
Drafts slide-by-slide carousel outlines for saveable educational posts.
Critiques drafts against weak hook, no escalation, and buried payoff failure modes.
Adapts recommendations for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts.
Provides honest guidance that avoids guaranteed viral performance claims.
limitations: Does not guarantee virality, views, saves, or revenue.
Does not access live platform analytics or current trend data.
Does not create finished videos, audio, thumbnails, or visual assets.
Requires the user to provide a topic, product, audience, or draft.

use_cases.title: Plan a Product Launch Video
use_cases.description: Generate multiple hooks and a tight short-form script for a new product, offer, or feature.

use_cases.title: Repurpose Long Content
use_cases.description: Turn a blog post, podcast, webinar, or long video into several distinct short-form ideas.
use_cases.target_user: Content teams and social media managers

use_cases.title: Improve Draft Performance
use_cases.description: Diagnose where a hook or script loses attention and rewrite the weak beats with clearer payoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Hook Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Give me 8 short-form hooks for [topic or product]. Label each hook by archetype and make each one concrete.

prompt_templates.title: Write a Short Script
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a 30-second [TikTok/Reels/Shorts] script about [topic]. Use hook, escalation, payoff, and CTA. Include on-screen text notes.

prompt_templates.title: Critique My Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Here is my draft: [paste draft]. Identify where attention drops, name the failure mode, and rewrite the weak hook and payoff.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Multi-Format Campaign
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn [source idea] into 5 distinct short-form pieces: talking head, demo, listicle, carousel, and POV. Give each a unique hook, structure, and platform note.

output_examples.input: Give me hooks for a video launching a $39 reusable cold-brew maker.
output: Withhold: I found the coffee gadget I would actually rebuy. I am not saying the brand yet.
Cost confession: I spent $400 on coffee gear so you can skip to the $39 one.
Contrarian: Your expensive coffee setup may be why it tastes worse.

output_examples.input: Write a 30-second talking-head script about why morning routines fail.
output: Hook: Your 5 AM routine may be why you crash by noon.
Escalation: You copied a routine built for someone else, so the timing fights your day.
Payoff: Build the routine around your energy pattern, then add one habit at a time.
CTA: Save this before you rebuild your schedule tonight.

output_examples.input: Turn a blog post about productivity myths into a carousel.
output: Slide 1: The productivity advice that quietly wastes your morning.
Slides 2-5: One myth per slide with a direct correction.
Slide 6: A saveable recap with the three changes to try first.
Slide 7: A simple save or follow CTA.

best_practices: Provide the audience, platform, topic, goal, and any brand constraints before asking for scripts.
Ask for several hook archetypes so the output explores different creative angles.
Check that every hook promise is paid off clearly inside the script or carousel.
anti_patterns: Asking for guaranteed viral content or specific view counts.
Using a brand-first introduction before the viewer understands the payoff.
Reusing the same hook angle across every short-form format.

faq.question: Does this skill guarantee a viral video?
faq.answer: No. It provides pattern-based guidance that can improve structure and clarity, but it cannot predict platform results.

faq.question: Which platforms does it support?
faq.answer: It supports TikTok, Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts, and carousel-style posts for Instagram or LinkedIn.

faq.question: Can it improve an existing draft?
faq.answer: Yes. Paste a hook, script, or carousel outline and ask for a critique against the retention failure modes.

faq.question: Does it use live trend or analytics data?
faq.answer: No. The skill uses general short-form content patterns and the context provided by the user.

faq.question: Can it create the final video file?
faq.answer: No. It creates written hooks, scripts, outlines, critique, and planning notes for production.

faq.question: What should I include in my prompt?
faq.answer: Include the topic, audience, platform, goal, desired length, and any claims or words to avoid.
